comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: CHECKPOINTED — not started. The last large structural refactor from the 2026-07-02 audit: group the reconciler (48 flat files) + llm/ into subpackages, and thread a TicketQuery dataclass through the 5-layer list_tickets replication. Deferred for a post-release maintenance pass per the agreed sequencing (correctness/governance first; wide dir-reorg import-churn is highest-risk to rush pre-release).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Plan-review: PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 12 advisory). Triage of the actionable findings (folded into execution):
- llm reorg (A.1) is NOT purely low-risk: (a) external consumer src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py imports 'from rebar.llm import eval' (L408,492) -> must update; (b) eval.py resolves Path(__file__).parent/'eval_specs' (L66) and fidelity_spot_eval.py uses files('rebar.llm').joinpath('eval_specs/...') -> if eval.py moves to evals/, its __file__ anchor + the importlib.resources anchors must be preserved (move eval_specs alongside or keep the anchor rooted at rebar.llm). Will handle explicitly.
- reconciler reorg (A.2) confirmed HIGH RISK by the reviewer (~15 path-load sites + sys.modules pre-seeding + Py3.14 __module__). Note: a _loader.py centralization already exists — will assess how much is already funnelled before deciding.
- Part B: will add a proving check for 'a new filter touches 3 sites not 6'.
Sequencing: Part B (TicketQuery) first as its own Gerrit change; then llm reorg; reconciler reorg last, pending a risk/benefit call.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Implementation status (worktree janitor/item-15-reorg-ticketquery):
- Part B (TicketQuery): DONE -> Gerrit change 235. list_states collapsed to (tracker, query); callers build TicketQuery; from_library owns None->'' + priority coercion; public/MCP/CLI scalar signatures unchanged; new test_ticket_query.py. Full suite 4329 passed.
- Part A.1 (llm reorg): DONE -> Gerrit change 238 (stacked on 235). Grouped rebar.llm root into prompting/ (prompts, prompt_library, prompts_frontmatter) + evals/ (eval, eval_scorers, eval_solver). Data dirs (reviewers/, eval_specs/) stay at rebar.llm; the two module-relative resource anchors re-rooted at rebar.llm; CI prompt-index command + docs updated; no __init__ re-exports (preserves the lazy-import contract). 61 files, all refs updated. make lint+check green; full suite 4336 passed.
- Part A.2 (reconciler reorg): DESCOPED with user agreement (2026-07-03). Rationale: the reconciler is path-loaded (spec_from_file_location under flat rebar_reconciler.<name> keys + sys.modules test pre-seeding + Py3.14 __module__ coupling), so grouping into acli/outbound/inbound would require rewriting ~15 loader sites + their tests in lockstep — HIGH RISK — for a package that is subprocess-isolated (absent from the import SCC), i.e. cosmetic tidiness with ZERO import-health benefit. Mirrors the audit's rejection of item 8 (allowlist governance). The 48-flat-file reconciler stays a RECORD, serviced by periodic maintenance.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
All ITEM 15 code MERGED to main (2026-07-03):
- Part B (TicketQuery): change 235 MERGED.
- llm reorg (prompting/ + evals/): change 238 MERGED (after: workflow-from-main drift-gate coupling fixed via change 240 'make drift gate tolerant of both module paths' MERGED; a pre-existing gitleaks false-positive on a fake test signing key in test_attestation_invalidation.py allowlisted in .gitleaks.toml; and a rebase to resolve the workflow-file conflict with 240).
- Reconciler reorg (A.2): DESCOPED with user agreement (high-risk path-loading rewrite for a subprocess-isolated package = cosmetic-only). Recorded above.
All three Gerrit changes passed LLM-Review +1 + Verified +1. Discovered a pre-existing e2e editor-test failure on main (bug fe55, linked). Closing.

compiled_state.description:
# Directory reorg + TicketQuery for the list_tickets 14-param replication

## Context
Two independent maintainability items from the 2026-07-02 audit, grouped under one ticket. They
share no code and land as SEPARATE Gerrit changes (each carries this ticket's trailer):
- **Part B (TicketQuery)** — collapse the ~14-filter replication that `list_tickets` threads across
  6 sites down to 3 boundary signatures + one dataclass.
- **Part A (dir reorg)** — group two flat packages into subpackages along existing prefix seams
  (reorg only, no file-splitting): the root `llm/` (normal imports; low risk) and the reconciler
  `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` (48 flat files; **path-loaded**, higher risk — see the note).

## Part B — TicketQuery (do first; contained)
`list_tickets` replicates the same ~14 filter params across 6 sites:
1. `src/rebar/__init__.py` — public facade `list_tickets` (scalar API; KEEP signature)
2. `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` — MCP tool `list_tickets` (tool schema; KEEP signature)
3. `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py` `_cmd_list` (argv contract; KEEP)
4. `src/rebar/_reads.py` `list_tickets` (internal pass-through — COLLAPSE)
5. `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py` `list_states` (filter core — COLLAPSE)
6. `src/rebar/reducer/_filters.py` `apply_ticket_filters` (7-param subset — partial)
Introduce a `TicketQuery` dataclass built at the 3 public boundaries and threaded inward via
`**asdict` / a `to_engine_kwargs()` that owns the `full`→`include_body` rename and None→"" coercion.
A new filter then touches 3 sites, not 6. Public + MCP + CLI signatures unchanged.

## Part A.1 — llm/ reorg (low risk)
Group by existing prefix seam (normal imports; update import paths only):
- `prompting/`: `prompts.py`, `prompt_library.py`, `prompts_frontmatter.py`
- `evals/`: `eval.py`, `eval_scorers.py`, `eval_solver.py` (fold in the existing `eval_specs/`)

## Part A.2 — reconciler reorg (HIGH RISK — path-loaded, subprocess-isolated)
`_engine/rebar_reconciler/` is NOT normal-imported: `__main__.py` loads siblings via
`spec_from_file_location` under **flat** dotted keys `rebar_reconciler.<name>`, computing
`Path(__file__).parent / f"{name}.py"`. ~15 sibling-load sites duplicate this, tests **pre-seed
`sys.modules` with these exact flat keys** as patch() targets, and Python 3.14 dataclass resolution
requires each `@dataclass` module's `__module__` to match its sys.modules key. Grouping into
`acli/`/`outbound/`/`inbound/` breaks every one of these unless the key + relative-path computation
and all pre-seeding tests are updated in lockstep. This package is subprocess-isolated (absent from
the import SCC), so the reorg yields tidiness only — no import-health benefit. Sequence LAST; if the
risk/benefit proves unfavorable, surface the tradeoff rather than force it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `TicketQuery` threaded through `_reads.list_tickets` + `list_states` (+ `apply_ticket_filters` where applicable); a new filter touches 3 sites not 6; public + MCP + CLI scalar signatures unchanged; tests green.
- [ ] `llm/` grouped into `prompting/` + `evals/`; imports updated; suites green.
- [ ] Reconciler grouped into `acli/`/`outbound/`/`inbound/` with the path-loader keys + relative paths + sys.modules-preseeding tests updated in lockstep and the subprocess boundary intact; suites green — OR the reorg is descoped with the risk/benefit tradeoff recorded and agreed.
- [ ] Each part lands as its own Gerrit change; `make lint` + `make check` + full suite green; commits carry `rebar-ticket:` trailers.
compiled_state.title: Directory reorg + TicketQuery for the list_tickets 14-param replication
